description: UI Bridge State Machine page — visual editor for discovering, creating, and managing state machines from UI Bridge-instrumented applications. Covers the full state machine creation lifecycle: SDK connection, state discovery (element co-occurrence analysis), graph editing, transition creation with action sequences, pathfinding (Dijkstra/BFS/A*), and export. The page is the primary interface for the qontinui library's state discovery algorithms, and this spec defines both UI correctness and algorithm output correctness.

groups.description: Phase 3 of discovery: running the state discovery algorithm on collected renders, previewing discovered states with confidence scores, naming the configuration, and saving to the project. The discovery phase calls POST /api/v1/state-discovery/ui-bridge/discover-states with the renders and strategy='auto'.

groups.description: After running state discovery, the page displays the discovered states. Each state represents a distinct UI configuration defined by which interactive elements appear together (co-occurrence). The correctness of the grouping algorithm is verifiable by examining the discovery results and the states displayed in the Graph Editor and State View tabs after saving.
groups.name: State Discovery — Grouping Correctness

groups.description: The Pathfinding tab allows users to select 'from' states (currently active) and 'target' states (to reach), then find the optimal transition path using the multistate library's pathfinding algorithms. Results show step-by-step transitions with costs. Found paths can be highlighted on the Graph Editor.
groups.name: Pathfinding

groups.description: End-to-end creation of a state machine: (1) connect to SDK app via runner, (2) collect renders by exploration or recording, (3) run discovery algorithm, (4) preview and name the configuration, (5) save to project. After saving, the configuration appears in the config selector dropdown and can be edited in Graph Editor.
groups.name: State Machine Creation Flow

groups.description: Semantic specification of the state machine data model as visible on the page. Each state has: id, name, element_ids (UI Bridge element identifiers), confidence score. Each transition has: from_states, activate_states, exit_states, actions list, path_cost, stays_visible. The discovery algorithm determines states by element co-occurrence patterns.
groups.name: State Machine Data Model

groups.description: The state discovery algorithm (qontinui core library's FingerprintStrategy) classifies elements by type and position, then groups them into states using co-occurrence analysis. This group specifies the algorithm's classification and grouping behavior as visible in the discovery results displayed on the page.
groups.name: Discovery Algorithm — Element Classification & Co-occurrence

groups.description: The SDK Recording Panel provides WebSocket-based fingerprint recording for UI Bridge-connected apps. groups.description: A floating recording indicator appears in the bottom-right corner of the app when any UI Bridge recording session is active. It polls SDK WebSocket ports and provides a quick stop button.
groups.name: Floating Recording Indicator

states.description: Core structural elements of the State Machine page: heading with Network icon, config selector dropdown, refresh button, and tab navigation bar with 6 tabs.
states.name: Page Structure

states.description: The page provides 6 tabs for different aspects of state machine management: Discovery, Graph Editor, State View, Transitions, Pathfinding, and Export. Each tab has an icon and label, rendered as plain buttons with active/inactive styling.
states.name: Tab Navigation

states.description: Style guide for the page header and tab navigation bar. The header uses border-b border-border-primary with bg-surface-primary background. Tab bar sits below the header with matching styling.
states.name: Visual Design — Header & Navigation

states.description: Ensures all text elements on the page are visible and readable against their backgrounds. This is a known recurring issue — foreground colors sometimes match background colors, making text invisible.
states.name: Text Visibility & Contrast

states.description: When no project is selected in the automation store, all tabs display a centered message instructing the user to select a project. The page header and tabs remain visible.
states.name: No Project Selected State

states.description: When a project is selected but no configuration is chosen, tabs that require a config should show clear instructive empty states. Discovery tab shows its collection UI instead.

elements.textContent: Select a configuration to view the state graph
Select a configuration to view states
Select a configuration to view transitions
Select a configuration to use pathfinding

states.name: No Configuration Selected State

states.description: Phase 1 of discovery: connecting to a runner and finding SDK-instrumented apps. The user selects a runner from a dropdown (local runner auto-detected), scans for apps, and connects to one. Shows a green banner when connected.
states.name: Discovery — Runner Connection

states.description: Phase 2 of discovery: collecting render snapshots from the connected SDK app via automated exploration (Explore tab) or manual recording (Record tab). Collection tabs only appear when an SDK app is connected.
states.name: Discovery — Data Collection

states.description: Phase 3 of discovery: running the state discovery algorithm on collected renders, previewing discovered states with confidence scores, naming the configuration, and saving to the project. The discovery phase calls POST /api/v1/state-discovery/ui-bridge/discover-states with the renders and strategy='auto'.
states.name: Discovery — State Discovery & Save

states.description: After discovery completes, the results card shows discovered state count, element count, render count, strategy badge, and a scrollable preview list of states with confidence scores.
states.name: Discovery — Results Preview

states.description: After running state discovery, the page displays the discovered states. Each state represents a distinct UI configuration defined by which interactive elements appear together (co-occurrence). The correctness of the grouping algorithm is verifiable by examining the discovery results and the states displayed in the Graph Editor and State View tabs after saving.
states.name: State Discovery — Grouping Correctness

states.description: Discovery state (renders, discovery results, config name) is persisted to localStorage per project. Navigating away and returning to the Discovery tab should restore the previous discovery state.
states.name: Discovery — State Persistence

states.description: The Graph Editor tab provides a ReactFlow-based visual canvas showing states as nodes and transitions as edges. Users can select nodes/edges, create transitions via the Add Transition button or by dragging elements between state nodes, and view state/transition details in side panels.
states.name: Graph Editor

states.description: Each state is rendered as a ReactFlow node showing the state name, element grid with type-colored tiles, confidence badge, element count, and connection indicators. The initial state shows a gold 'START' badge.
states.name: Graph — State Nodes

states.description: When a state is selected in the Graph Editor, a side panel (w-80, border-l, bg-surface-primary) shows editable state details: name, description, state ID, confidence, elements list, render count, and domain knowledge.
states.name: State Detail Panel

states.description: The State View tab shows a list/grid of all states in the selected configuration using the shared StateViewPanel component from @qontinui/workflow-ui/state-machine. Allows browsing states and their elements without the graph complexity.
states.name: State View Tab

states.description: The transition editor panel allows creating and editing transitions with: name, from/activate/exit states (toggle buttons with color coding), action sequence with 19+ action types, path cost, and stays_visible flag. Opens as a w-80 side panel in the Graph Editor.
states.name: Transition Editor

states.description: Transitions support 19+ action types that define what happens during state changes. Each action type has specific parameters. These map to the UIBridgeRuntime action handlers in the qontinui library.
states.name: Transition — Action Types

states.description: The Transitions tab shows a searchable, filterable list of all transitions with action type icons, from/to state labels, and an action playback visualization with play/pause controls.

elements.textContent: Select a transition to view its details

states.name: Transitions List Panel

states.description: The Pathfinding tab allows users to select 'from' states (currently active) and 'target' states (to reach), then find the optimal transition path using the multistate library's pathfinding algorithms. Results show step-by-step transitions with costs. Found paths can be highlighted on the Graph Editor.
states.name: Pathfinding

states.description: The Export tab allows downloading the state machine as JSON (compatible with UIBridgeRuntime.from_dict()) or pushing it directly to the qontinui-runner. Shows config name, state count, and transition count.
states.name: Export

states.description: End-to-end creation of a state machine: (1) connect to SDK app via runner, (2) collect renders by exploration or recording, (3) run discovery algorithm, (4) preview and name the configuration, (5) save to project. After saving, the configuration appears in the config selector dropdown and can be edited in Graph Editor.
states.name: State Machine Creation Flow

states.description: Semantic specification of the state machine data model as visible on the page. Each state has: id, name, element_ids (UI Bridge element identifiers), confidence score. Each transition has: from_states, activate_states, exit_states, actions list, path_cost, stays_visible. The discovery algorithm determines states by element co-occurrence patterns.
states.name: State Machine Data Model

states.description: Style guide for the discovery panel components: cards, badges, buttons, and connection status indicators.
states.name: Visual Design — Discovery Panel

states.description: Elements can be dragged between state nodes in the Graph Editor to create transitions or reassign elements. The useElementDrag hook manages drag state, drop targets, and resulting operations.
states.name: Graph — Element Drag & Drop

states.description: The State View tab uses the shared StateViewPanel component which provides dual view modes (list and spatial canvas), search/filter, element grouping by type with colored badges, confidence color coding, domain knowledge display, acceptance criteria, and transition counts per state.
states.name: State View — Rich Features

states.description: The Transitions tab uses the shared TransitionsPanel which includes a full action playback animation system. When a transition with actions is selected, controls allow playing, pausing, stepping through, and adjusting speed of an animated visualization of each action in the sequence.
states.name: Transitions — Action Playback Animation

states.description: The StateDetailPanel (shown as a side panel when a state is selected in the Graph Editor) provides editing for state metadata, acceptance criteria, domain knowledge, and element management with change detection.
states.name: State Detail — Editing Features

states.description: The Graph Editor (StateMachineGraphView) supports keyboard shortcuts for navigation, layout, and selection. A '?' toggle (top-right) reveals the shortcut overlay listing all available keys.
states.name: Graph — Keyboard Shortcuts

states.description: The state discovery algorithm (qontinui core library's FingerprintStrategy) classifies elements by type and position, then groups them into states using co-occurrence analysis. This group specifies the algorithm's classification and grouping behavior as visible in the discovery results displayed on the page.
states.name: Discovery Algorithm — Element Classification & Co-occurrence

states.description: The SDK Recording Panel provides WebSocket-based fingerprint recording for UI Bridge-connected apps. It captures user interactions, computes element fingerprints, and produces a CooccurrenceExport for direct state machine bootstrapping — bypassing the snapshot-based discovery flow.
states.name: Fingerprint-Based Recording (SdkRecordingPanel)

states.description: After fingerprint-based recording pipeline produces results, a compact graph visualization shows discovered states as nodes and transitions as edges. Uses @xyflow/react with dagre layout.
states.name: Recording State Graph Visualization

states.description: After pipeline processing with playbook generation enabled, the SdkRecordingPanel displays the generated playbook markdown content with a copy-to-clipboard button.
states.name: Playbook Generation from Recording

states.description: When connecting to an app with previous recording sessions, the SdkRecordingPanel shows past session history to inform the user and enable incremental recording.
states.name: Experience Memory (Past Recording Sessions)

states.description: The fingerprint recording pipeline (RecordingPipeline.process_recording) discovers states via FingerprintStateDiscovery and transitions via TransitionDetector. These assertions verify that the pipeline output displayed on the page is correct.
states.name: Recording Pipeline — State Discovery Correctness

states.description: The StateMachineViewer component now has a Graph tab as its first (default) tab, rendering an interactive state transition graph using @xyflow/react and dagre layout.
states.name: State Machine Graph Tab (StateMachineViewer)

states.description: A floating recording indicator appears in the bottom-right corner of the app when any UI Bridge recording session is active. It polls SDK WebSocket ports and provides a quick stop button.
states.name: Floating Recording Indicator
